465: Supports are: 6822 (major), 6818, 6813, 6808 (major), 6802, 6795 (major), 6790, 6782 (major), 6776, 6770 (major), 6765, 6759 (major), 6752, 6747, 6743 (major), 6736, 6732 (major), 6727, 6720 (major), 6711, 6704-6700 (major), 6695, 6690 (major), 6685, 6680 (major), 6672, 6663 (major), 6657, 6652 (major), 6646, 6639 (major), 6635, 6631 (major), 6626, 6620 (major), 6609, 6605, 6597 (major), 6593, 6588 (major), 6583, 6577, 6570 (major).
466: 
467: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am holding my 10% long runner from the 6742 Failed Breakdown we saw a little after 11am today, discussed above. We yet again had a monster squeeze today identical price action to yesterday (big overnight/morning sell, squeeze in the afternoon after a Failed Breakdown). This is very typical ES price action. Unfortunately though, readers know what I’m going to say now. After a major squeeze like this, there is nothing to trade. I am a Failed Breakdown trader. I wait for those scary, war/bad news driven elevator down sells. Then I wait for the Failed Breakdown, then I long. After the long, I don’t force trades. I wait for the next elevator down sell, and do it again. After a big run is where most losses occur because price needs to digest the move and this often means tons of traps, false starts, and noise. Wait for volatility. Generally though the big picture is unchanged: We are still stuck in the same range we’ve been in for weeks. It is constantly morphing and changing each day but it is mostly 6808 support (this was a big support Sunday evening, then it became resistance this afternoon, then cleared) to 6904. As always - and as I said yesterday - Everything in this range=chop. If you aren’t trading the Failed Breakdowns, if you are looking for home runs, if you think you can predict the action, and if you don’t manage level to level; you are thinking like a retail trader, and your account will gradually get transferred into institutional accounts as they trap you over and over. We saw that today. This morning when were selling my feed was full of people calling for a bear market. This is a retail mistake because they are thinking in straight lines AND predicting. As such, they were punished. As of writing 6822 is first support down. We already tested this several times this afternoon. Its risky to buy now. Instead of buying it, one possible entry is the Failed Breakdown of 6817 low we set at 2:30PM today. If we can trap below this perhaps down to 6808 then recover 6817, this is a lower quality long. It is fine if it goes down to 6795 also. Below 6795 we could take a trip back to the lows. There is some support en route at 6770 but instead of buying this wait for it to trap and recover (we set a low there at 11:30AM today). 6742 is the last support below there. If its a slow grind into it, one can try the bid here but remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed in ES. Most the time when ES sells off it goes elevator down and when this is happening ES does not care about supports until the sell algo turns off (usually takes a few minutes to a few hours). If ES is grinding slowly into 6742 one can try it, but if we are knifing, I’d skip this. Nothing below there until the Failed Breakdown of today’s 6718 daily low. Bonus if ES can tag 6700-04 on this.

raw_mancini_source.surrounding_source_context:
16: What does profit protection mode mean? This means after a win 1) I have to sit on the runner and do nothing all day, no other trades allowed. 2) I can take a second or third trade, only IF the runner stops out below break-even (or via a trailer if it is a larger rally where a trailing stop is involved), AND a new, pre-planned setup emerges. 3) I can take this second or third trade only risking the profits from the first, to ensure I have a green day. I take not going green to red on the day very seriously as do I take profit preservation very seriously.
17: 
18: We ran nicely to 6800+ after this, and runners paid heavily.
19: 
20: I do this trade recap section for one reason mostly - to give new traders a sense of what running a system looks like in reality because if new traders today are anything like I was starting off, they have a laughably wrong idea of what profitable trading looks like (and as a result, aren’t profitable).

raw_mancini_source.surrounding_source_context:
378: NOTE: The purpose of this trade recap section is to run down in greater detail previous examples of my three setup types that occurred within the last couple days. These setups are always provided to readers in advance, and the goal of this section is to expedite the lengthy screen time requirement that is associated with learning any trade setup. Trading is no different than learning a sport, or a musical instrument, or any other skill. You need to put in the reps - thousands of them. Unfortunately with trading, these reps are expensive. By showing examples of my three setups here everyday, the hope is readers can develop the pattern recognition quicker. The idea is that if there was a trade you were curious about or wanted more color on that occurred in the last couple days, you can scan through this section and hopefully I have identified it. Scroll to the next section for today’s action and the trade plan for tomorrow. Note that this section is also not exhaustive and I do not include every setup that occurred. When price is volatile, there may be many.

311: Trade Plan Tuesday
312: 
313: Supports are: 6778-82 (major), 6774, 6764, 6758 (major), 6752, 6747, 6739-42 (major), 6732, 6727, 6721, 6716 (major), 6707, 6700 (major), 6693 (major), 6682, 6676 (major), 6668, 6663 (major), 6657, 6652 (major), 6645, 6639, 6632 (major), 6623 (major), 6617, 6609-12 (major), 6605, 6597 (major), 6589, 6583, 6577, 6573 (major), 6559, 6554, 6542 (major), 6536, 6527 (major), 6522 (major), 6517, 6514, 6508, 6504 (major), 6499, 6492 (major), 6486, 6476 (major), 6468, 6457, 6450 (major).
314: 
315: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 6612 Failed Breakdown we saw at 12:10AM last evening and this long showed what Failed Breakdowns are all about. Last evening ES was going elevator down and collapsing. The world was ending on social media. I (and institutions, and readers of this newsletter) knew what was coming though, because its all I write about everyday. The Failed Breakdown and short squeeze. We got it, and rallied 150+ points. Readers know what I’m going to say here though - we just had a massive 150+ point long, and therefore there is nothing to do now. My job is to wait for those scary elevator down sells where retailers are panicking, social media is bearish. Then, I wait for the Failed Breakdown, and up we go. After the squeeze though, there is nothing for me to do but wait for the next “world is ending” sell and do it again, as I have thousands and thousands and thousands of times before. First support down is 6778-82. This was a huge resistance all Friday and we tested it over and over Friday before rejecting. Today, we cleared. While one could try a bid here, I won’t personally and I’ve got literally interest in longing after a big run like this (i am also already long in my runner). Remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed. If ES is collapsing full speed into 6778-82, instead of buying it at least wait for it to trap and recover. There is not much below there until 6739-42. This is another spot to try a bid and 6739-42 was a big support on Friday, and resistance at noon today before clearing. If we are knifing down, wait for it to flush to 6732 and recover to try long. Below there is 6700 and this is where things get interesting for me. At 1:45PM today ES set a huge low at ~6700 from which we rallied over 100 points into today’s high. The flush and recovery of this 6700 level is an obvious Failed Breakdown. Bonus if we can tag 6693 on this before heading back up. Below there 6676 is a support of interest, as is 6663 (watch for traps and recoveries of this). If we really retrace a good chunk of today’s rally, also watch for the Failed Breakdown of today’s 10am 6641 low and this would be a large Failed Breakdown.

mancini_source_text: A little after midnight, ES recovered that low (Failed Breakdown) and ripped to ~6800+ today.

raw_mancini_source.quote: From there? More weekend risk, and we went elevator down to 6588 by 10pm last night. Shortly after - a little after midnight - we got what always comes. A Failed Breakdown. Specifically right off the 6pm futures open Sunday ES sold to 6612 and bounced ~34 points. By 10pm, ES flushed that low down to 6588. A little after midnight, ES recovered that low (Failed Breakdown) and ripped to ~6800+ today.
raw_mancini_source.surrounding_source_context:
218: We saw it Friday morning. ES went elevator down from 6800+ to 6715.75 by 9:50AM. This was a fast, classic elevator down sell. What comes next? The short squeeze. By selling to 6715.75, ES lost Tuesday’s 6718.75 daily low. Shortly after, it recovered (Failed Breakdown) and the parabolic rip began to 6778-82 where we peaked Friday afternoon.
219: 
220: From there? More weekend risk, and we went elevator down to 6588 by 10pm last night. Shortly after - a little after midnight - we got what always comes. A Failed Breakdown. Specifically right off the 6pm futures open Sunday ES sold to 6612 and bounced ~34 points. By 10pm, ES flushed that low down to 6588. A little after midnight, ES recovered that low (Failed Breakdown) and ripped to ~6800+ today.
221: 
222: Elevator down, Failed Breakdown, Short Squeeze. The cycle repeats over and over.

184: 
185: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 10:45AM 6699 Failed Breakdown, discussed above and provided yesterday. Today was another extreme chop session and as I warned yesterday and again in the paragraphs above - this is only hard to trade if you are trading it. If you simply wait for the big elevator down sell into Failed Breakdown, lock in gains level to level, leave a runner. Maybe take a second trade maximum after only risking profits if that runner trails out, the action is easy. If you are flipping back and forth long and short though as price ping pongs in a 20 point range for 5 hours, you are going to lose. We need volatility for any trade to work. How do you know we have volatility? Price puts in a big, vertical selloff that leaves the chop range. After that, it is safe to trade. Heading into tomorrow though, ES finally broke the stubborn range it spent from Monday to yesterday stuck inside, and the task for bulls now is to recover back into it. This range is generally 6765 support and resistance up at 6815 and bulls need to get back in that in order to end the sell bounces theme we’ve had all day. First support down as of writing is 6685. This was a heavy support shelf today holding at 11:20AM for a 44 point bounce, and at 3pm for a 17 point bounce. Late day today, we flushed this shelf down to 6680 before the close, and the recovery of 6685 would constitute a Failed Breakdown. Below 6685 is 6663. While one can try a bid here, just rememeber my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed. Remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed. If ES is collapsing full speed into 6663 never buy. If its a slow, controlled sell into the zone one can try but if we are freefalling at least wait for it to flush and recover. This is especially important when bears control as supports become much weaker on average. Below 6663 is 6652. This is another decent support but if we are collapsing, the much safer entry is the Failed Breakdown of Monday’s 10:10AM 6641 low. This is a huge low from which we ran into this weeks highs and the Failed Breakdown is obviously actionable. Nothing below there until 6624. We set a huge low at 6624 on Monday at 3:05AM and flushes and recoveries of this are actionable. If it can tag 6612 and recover 6624, this is very high quality. If 6612 fails we probably make new lows. I’d watch for a big Failed Breakdown of Sunday’s 6585. If we can tag 6571 and recover this, its an excellent trade.

37: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from Sunday evenings 6689 Failed Breakdown. My most recent entry was 6735 Failed Breakdown entry that I took this morning at 7:30AM, discussed above. Please read the FOMC guide at the start of this newsletter as well, this is key for trading tomorrow. First support down heading into tomorrow is 6770. We literally spent all day here today and at this point it is a heavily used up level, I won’t be longing it directly. A much cleaner alternative is to wait for a Failed Breakdown of today’s 6764 noon low. This was a significant low as we held it and rallied 22 points so the flush and recovery would be actionable. Below there is 6749 and this is a strong support cluster. However, it is important to remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed. Most the time when ES sells it is going elevator down and I never, ever catch a knife. When a sell algo is not no support will hold my definition. If its a controlled, gradual sell into 6749 one can bid it but if we are knifing full speed after FOMC, wait for it to defend and recover 6764 to put in a Failed Breakdown. Below 6749 is 6727. Also a decent support on a normal day, but if we are plunging on FOMC, don’t touch it. Here, the safer entry is the Failed Breakdown of the 2am 6716 low. This was today’s daily low from which we rallied 93 points and if we can flush and recover this it is an obvious Failed Breakdown. Bonus if we can tag 6703 on this. Sweeps and recoveries of 6703 are also actionable. There is not much under there until 6673 is and this the sort of last ditch support for bulls and below there we should take out last weeks lows. The only entries below there are of interest would be 1) The Failed Breakdown of Sunday’s opening 6658 low and 2) The Failed Breakdown of last weeks 6635 low, bonus if we can hit 6623 on this. 6593 is the only support of interest under there.

The non-acceptance protocol typically triggers for shallow (under 10 point flush of the significant low) and fast Failed Breakdowns, but you can use it for any Failed Breakdown. Typically I always use it when there is not clear, obvious, “jumps out at you” acceptance.

134: 
135: In this case though, we had very clear evidence of the first type of acceptance shown in the above chart. Remember the first form price back-tests the significant low (6716) from below, sells off, then returns to it. By doing this (selling off at the significant low then rebounding back to it), price tells us there is no supply at the significant low and we can safetly long above when it recovers by a few points. In this case ES backtested 6716 at 11pm, sold 10 points, returned to it at 12:15AM, sold 10 points, then popped through around 1am and one could long at ~6722 or so (I always like to give several buffer points especially if there is lots of noise around the level). You are always better entering higher away from the noise.

466: 
467: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am holding my 10% long runner from the 6742 Failed Breakdown we saw a little after 11am today, discussed above. We yet again had a monster squeeze today identical price action to yesterday (big overnight/morning sell, squeeze in the afternoon after a Failed Breakdown). This is very typical ES price action. Unfortunately though, readers know what I’m going to say now. After a major squeeze like this, there is nothing to trade. I am a Failed Breakdown trader. I wait for those scary, war/bad news driven elevator down sells. Then I wait for the Failed Breakdown, then I long. After the long, I don’t force trades. I wait for the next elevator down sell, and do it again. After a big run is where most losses occur because price needs to digest the move and this often means tons of traps, false starts, and noise. Wait for volatility. Generally though the big picture is unchanged: We are still stuck in the same range we’ve been in for weeks. It is constantly morphing and changing each day but it is mostly 6808 support (this was a big support Sunday evening, then it became resistance this afternoon, then cleared) to 6904. As always - and as I said yesterday - Everything in this range=chop. If you aren’t trading the Failed Breakdowns, if you are looking for home runs, if you think you can predict the action, and if you don’t manage level to level; you are thinking like a retail trader, and your account will gradually get transferred into institutional accounts as they trap you over and over. We saw that today. This morning when were selling my feed was full of people calling for a bear market. This is a retail mistake because they are thinking in straight lines AND predicting. As such, they were punished. As of writing 6822 is first support down. We already tested this several times this afternoon. Its risky to buy now. Instead of buying it, one possible entry is the Failed Breakdown of 6817 low we set at 2:30PM today. If we can trap below this perhaps down to 6808 then recover 6817, this is a lower quality long. It is fine if it goes down to 6795 also. Below 6795 we could take a trip back to the lows. There is some support en route at 6770 but instead of buying this wait for it to trap and recover (we set a low there at 11:30AM today). 6742 is the last support below there. If its a slow grind into it, one can try the bid here but remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed in ES. Most the time when ES sells off it goes elevator down and when this is happening ES does not care about supports until the sell algo turns off (usually takes a few minutes to a few hours). If ES is grinding slowly into 6742 one can try it, but if we are knifing, I’d skip this. Nothing below there until the Failed Breakdown of today’s 6718 daily low. Bonus if ES can tag 6700-04 on this.

37: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from Sunday evenings 6689 Failed Breakdown. My most recent entry was 6735 Failed Breakdown entry that I took this morning at 7:30AM, discussed above. Please read the FOMC guide at the start of this newsletter as well, this is key for trading tomorrow. First support down heading into tomorrow is 6770. We literally spent all day here today and at this point it is a heavily used up level, I won’t be longing it directly. A much cleaner alternative is to wait for a Failed Breakdown of today’s 6764 noon low. This was a significant low as we held it and rallied 22 points so the flush and recovery would be actionable. Below there is 6749 and this is a strong support cluster. However, it is important to remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed. Most the time when ES sells it is going elevator down and I never, ever catch a knife. When a sell algo is not no support will hold my definition. If its a controlled, gradual sell into 6749 one can bid it but if we are knifing full speed after FOMC, wait for it to defend and recover 6764 to put in a Failed Breakdown. Below 6749 is 6727. Also a decent support on a normal day, but if we are plunging on FOMC, don’t touch it. Here, the safer entry is the Failed Breakdown of the 2am 6716 low. This was today’s daily low from which we rallied 93 points and if we can flush and recover this it is an obvious Failed Breakdown. Bonus if we can tag 6703 on this. Sweeps and recoveries of 6703 are also actionable. There is not much under there until 6673 is and this the sort of last ditch support for bulls and below there we should take out last weeks lows. The only entries below there are of interest would be 1) The Failed Breakdown of Sunday’s opening 6658 low and 2) The Failed Breakdown of last weeks 6635 low, bonus if we can hit 6623 on this. 6593 is the only support of interest under there.

615: 
616: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 10:45AM monster 6808 Failed Breakdown we had Tuesday. My most recent entry was the 6867-70 Level Reclaim long we had at 3:45PM today, which was managed level to level and tweeted out live at 3:30PM (shown above). I will warn tomorrow is OPEX day. While not always the case, OPEX days are often choppy, poor quality trading (much like today). Not an issue as long as you don’t overtrade, trade the Failed Breakdowns, don’t chase, and manage gains level to level. Today was mostly a consolidation session. After rallying 130 points on Tuesday/Wednesday, ES spent today in consolidation digesting it. The range is mostly 6845-6884 now, with 6867-60 being a key pivot inside. As long as price is stuck in this, expect brutal, slow, grinding chop with bad follow through (lots of quick fake outs that go one level and reverse). Level to level trading is key. 6861 is first support down. This has been a heavily choppy region today. Its quite risky to buy now if you want to trade it its best to wait for it to hold and recover 6866-70 which has been a huge magnet all day. This is a Level Reclaim NOT a Failed Breakdown. Remember Level Reclaims occur when price recovers a horizontal support/resistance line. Failed Breakowns occur when price flushes and recovers a clear significant low. You can see between 5:45AM and 9:30AM we tapped this zone numerous times as support before trading through all afternoon, but you can still extend a line from 5:45AM to 9:30AM rightwards. Note this trade already triggered late day before the newsletter came out and I tweeted it live at 3:30PM (screenshot above). It should still be good if we get it again though. If 6861 fails there is a good chance ES takes out today’s lows. 6845 is next big support. Remember my core rule: No knife catching allowed in ES. Most the time when ES sells off it goes elevator down and when this is happening ES does not care about supports until the sell algo turns off (usually takes a few minutes to a few hours). We’ve seen this daily nearly. If we are knifing fast - instead of buying 6845 - the very obvious trade here is the Failed Breakdown of today’s 6848 daily low. Wait for 6845 or 6838 to hold then recover 6848. There is nothing below there until 6822. If its a controlled sell into the region, one can try a bid there. If we are collapsing full speed (there is alot of geopolitical risk now RE: Iran, so if we are getting headlines about that, don’t buy any supports). Instead, wait for ES to hold 6813 and recover 6822. There is nothing below there until the Failed Breakdown of the 6791 daily low. Bonus if we can tag 6782 on this then recover 6791. I like 6772 and 6758 below there.

As readers know I don’t short ES - I only get my points on the long side, but I still give short entries here for those who like them and don’t mind having a substantially lower win rate and lower R/R - SPX is a structurally bullish asset class green 75% of years over the last 100 years with a 1% annualized monthly return and even in bear markets it is extremely hard to short since the sells are short lived, news driven (often random) and resolve in violent rip. Since we broke down a big shelf last Thursday up at 6819 though, there are some backtest shorts available. One would be 6815-19. Just be careful however if it is after FOMC ES can easily spike 100+ points in a few minutes. On a normal day thoiugh, this is a spot to try. Above there, 6840 is a spot to try as is 6854 and 6882.

raw_mancini_source.quote: The question for today was if bulls could keep this bounce going to backtest towards 6819. My lean was they could. I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “The bull case for tomorrow is that ES can keep up the momentum from last evenings 6689-90 Failed Breakdown, and run to backtest 6819. I discussed entries for this leg above, but in a strong bull case, ES will hold 6743 (or quick trap below 6735) then head up direct.” We got right to work here. Last evening, we trapped below 6735, recovered, and ripped to 6808 high of day.
raw_mancini_source.surrounding_source_context:
8: The task for bulls this week if they wanted a rally would therefore be twofold. 1) To start a squeeze, they’d need to put in a Failed Breakdown and to do this, theyd need to recover 6689 which was a big shelf all day Friday. 2) If they wanted to sustain a rally, they’d need to backtest then recover 6819. I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “My general lean is ES can try and recover 6689 to generate a rally to 6743, 6754, 6766, then 6819” We got started right on this after 6pm Sunday and by 7pm we recovered 6689 and ran to 6743+ yesterday, exactly per plan.

6: This what we had at 6819 early last week, tested countless times. It is also why I wrote last Wednesday at 4pm: “Bear Case Thursday: Begins on the failure of 6819”. Thursday morning, price picked its path, and down we went going elevator down. Readers know however that ES does not sell in straight lines. Every elevator down sell sees an equally violent squeeze the other way. Those squeezes however, only occur when price triggers my core setup: The Failed Breakdown which occurs when price flushes a major low, traps shorts, then reverses.

I discussed entries for this leg above, but in a strong bull case, ES will hold 6743 (or quick trap below 6735) then head up direct.” We got right to work here. Last evening, we trapped below 6735, recovered, and ripped to 6808 high of day.

3281: 
3282: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 10:10AM 6638 Failed Breakdown. Today was another session of tactical, level to level trading. We remain in what I call a Mode 2 range. Remember price can only do two things. It can trend (Mode 1, this is only 10% of sessions) or it can consolidate (Mode 2, this is 90% of sessions). Mode 2 refers to rangebound price action where price is contained in a consolidation, and does not move in straight line from open to close. It moves on way for a few minutes or hours, traps, reverses, does the same the other way, ultimately just to close the day inside the range. In Mode 2, Failed Breakdowns are highly prevalent especially below range support). Mode 2 is not hard to trade as long as you follow the rules. 1) Do not predict what price is going to do, wait on planned Failed Breakdowns and react 2) Never expect home runs. Manage level to level, and leave a runner. This range is morphing day to day but heading into tomorrow it is mostly 6592 support with 6678 resistance now. Inside this range, 6623 and 6638 are key pivots. 6638 was a magnet and heavily traded support shelf all day. Reclaims of this level remain actionable and we are sitting a little below it as of writing. This has already played out many times today but still remains actionable. 6623 is below there. This is a very well tested support now, instead of buying it, a much safer trade is the Failed Breakdown of today’s 6616 daily low. 6604 is support below there. If its a slow grind into this support, one can try the bid there but remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed. Most the time when ES sells it goes elevator down and this means flushing through every support for a few minutes to a few hours like they don’t exist. An elevator down sell will retrace a rally in a fraction of time the rally took. If we are crashing full speed into 6604 instead of buying it wait for it to hold and put in a Failed Breakdown of that 6616 daily low. Below 6604 we head back to 6592 which remains range support. While one could give this a bid, its much safer just to wait for it to defend then recover 6604 to get long. Below 6592 we probably crash. I wuld be cautious with longs. There is no support until 6575. Rather than buying this, we set a huge low there yesterday (it was the low of day) and flushes and recoveries here are actionable. Below there, 6542 and 6524 are supports of interest

2928: 
2929: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from yesterday’s 6593 Failed Breakdown that occurred at 11am Tuesday and this evolved into a staggering 250+ point long and this is exactly why I hold runners. We had another massive run today so readers know what I’m going to say here. My job as a Failed Breakdown trader is to wait for those scary elevator down sells where people are panicking, calling tops, chasing short. Then, I long when a low/shelf of lows recovers. Then, we rip, and I hold runners. After we rip - my work is done. I simply don’t trade until we get another Failed Breakdown. I don’t chase, don’t force trades, don’t trade chop. As readers know, after a major structural breakout (like we saw of the big white flag from February shown in my below chart that occurred Tuesday) setups often become scarce. This is fine - it’s what runners are for. Price tends to give tons and tons of opportunities to get in prior to these moves, but once it breaks, it breaks. Today, ES broke out another small flag which we built from Tuesday’s high into this morning. Generally resistance of this is 6821-26 now (this was the region we lifted off from this morning) and bulls want to hold it or quick trap below on any dips tomorrow. As of writing though, 6848 is first support down. At 2:35PM today, we set a nice 18 point low there. I would not buy 6848 directly now, but if we can flush that low and recover it would be a lower quality Failed Breakdown. Below 6848 ES likely flushes down to 6821-26. While one could just give this a bid, remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed in ES. If we are plunging full speed on some headline, never get infront. If we are knifing at full speed the much safer entry is to wait for a Failed Breakdown. In this case there are no significant lows nearby, but if we can flush and then recover 6826 this may be an entry. 6809 is below there and this is also a decent support. Below there is the first possible Failed Breakdown. Specifically, at ~10:30AM today, ES set a clear significant low at 6802 from which we rallied to today’s highs. The flush of this low and recovery is a quality Failed Breakdown (ideal if it tags 6793 in the process). 6780 is below there. This was yesterday’s (Wednesday’s) low of day. A flush and recovery of this low is a clear Failed Breakdown and a powerful one. Below there 6756 is a support to watch.

Bulls did what they had to do though - they bought the dip. On Monday evening at 830PM, ES put in a massive Failed Breakdown where we swept the Sunday/Monday regular hours shelf of lows at 6360 down to 6353. We then rallied ~290 points into this weeks highs. After a big squeeze like this, a dip is inevitable, but the question is whether or not that dip gets bought on the first pullback. Today it did, we sold down to 6506, recovered a big low at 6520 set at 2am around 9:30AM, and ripped. With the dip bought, the task for bulls now is to keep the momentum going and we will hold runners. As of writing 6604 is first support down. This is a sloppy, messy zone now and not one I would engage. If we can flush it down to 6592 and recover though. This is actionable. 6592 remains an extremely important support line in its own right. It was support multiple times on Wednesday, failed last evening, then has been support today since noon. Instead of just buying it, the Failed Breakdown of 6592 is actionable. Note to be precise, there is 12:20PM low at 6586 and a 2pm low at 6593. Ideally you want both these flushed and recovered. If 6592 fails its back down the levels and there is not much until 6553. If its a controlled sell into this region, one can bid it. But remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed in ES. If we are plunging full speed on some headline, never get infront. The safer alternative is to wait for 6553 to defend and recover the first level up which is 6563. Below 6553 we taken another dive. 6525-30 is a weaker support but its a high risk long. The strongest support is 6499. While one could bid this, a much clearer entry is the Failed Breakdown of today’s daily low which is located at 6505. If ES can flush 6505 down to 6499 and recover 6505, this is a strong entry. 6484, and flushes/recoveries of 6447 are of interest below there.

2929: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from yesterday’s 6593 Failed Breakdown that occurred at 11am Tuesday and this evolved into a staggering 250+ point long and this is exactly why I hold runners. We had another massive run today so readers know what I’m going to say here. My job as a Failed Breakdown trader is to wait for those scary elevator down sells where people are panicking, calling tops, chasing short. Then, I long when a low/shelf of lows recovers. Then, we rip, and I hold runners. After we rip - my work is done. I simply don’t trade until we get another Failed Breakdown. I don’t chase, don’t force trades, don’t trade chop. As readers know, after a major structural breakout (like we saw of the big white flag from February shown in my below chart that occurred Tuesday) setups often become scarce. This is fine - it’s what runners are for. Price tends to give tons and tons of opportunities to get in prior to these moves, but once it breaks, it breaks. Today, ES broke out another small flag which we built from Tuesday’s high into this morning. Generally resistance of this is 6821-26 now (this was the region we lifted off from this morning) and bulls want to hold it or quick trap below on any dips tomorrow. As of writing though, 6848 is first support down. At 2:35PM today, we set a nice 18 point low there. I would not buy 6848 directly now, but if we can flush that low and recover it would be a lower quality Failed Breakdown. Below 6848 ES likely flushes down to 6821-26. While one could just give this a bid, remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed in ES. If we are plunging full speed on some headline, never get infront. If we are knifing at full speed the much safer entry is to wait for a Failed Breakdown. In this case there are no significant lows nearby, but if we can flush and then recover 6826 this may be an entry. 6809 is below there and this is also a decent support. Below there is the first possible Failed Breakdown. Specifically, at ~10:30AM today, ES set a clear significant low at 6802 from which we rallied to today’s highs. The flush of this low and recovery is a quality Failed Breakdown (ideal if it tags 6793 in the process). 6780 is below there. This was yesterday’s (Wednesday’s) low of day. A flush and recovery of this low is a clear Failed Breakdown and a powerful one. Below there 6756 is a support to watch.

3167: Then overnight Sunday into Monday? The cycle repeats again and we drifted lower into 6485 by 3:30AM. This was less of an elevator down and more of a grind lower as the market was tired. Nonetheless, the results were the same. Around 4am Monday, ES bounced and recovered the 6505 Sunday opening low. We pushed higher to 6623 again, then around 7am a Trump Tweet sent the market higher 200+ points to 6700+ high Monday. While we can’t predict when headlines will hit, we can take every Failed Breakdown that we are at the desk for, and let the normal distribution of returns play out. raw_mancini_source.quote: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 9:28AM 6525 Failed Breakdown we saw today, discussed above. Also note that markets are closed tomorrow for Good Friday, meaning this plan is for Monday. We had a massive rip today in ES but we ultimately closed not far from where we did yesterday. Bulls did what they had to do though - they bought the dip. On Monday evening at 830PM, ES put in a massive Failed Breakdown where we swept the Sunday/Monday regular hours shelf of lows at 6360 down to 6353. We then rallied ~290 points into this weeks highs. After a big squeeze like this, a dip is inevitable, but the question is whether or not that dip gets bought on the first pullback. Today it did, we sold down to 6506, recovered a big low at 6520 set at 2am around 9:30AM, and ripped. With the dip bought, the task for bulls now is to keep the momentum going and we will hold runners. As of writing 6604 is first support down. This is a sloppy, messy zone now and not one I would engage. If we can flush it down to 6592 and recover though. This is actionable. 6592 remains an extremely important support line in its own right. It was support multiple times on Wednesday, failed last evening, then has been support today since noon. Instead of just buying it, the Failed Breakdown of 6592 is actionable. Note to be precise, there is 12:20PM low at 6586 and a 2pm low at 6593. Ideally you want both these flushed and recovered. If 6592 fails its back down the levels and there is not much until 6553. If its a controlled sell into this region, one can bid it. But remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed in ES. If we are plunging full speed on some headline, never get infront. The safer alternative is to wait for 6553 to defend and recover the first level up which is 6563. Below 6553 we taken another dive. 6525-30 is a weaker support but its a high risk long. The strongest support is 6499. While one could bid this, a much clearer entry is the Failed Breakdown of today’s daily low which is located at 6505. If ES can flush 6505 down to 6499 and recover 6505, this is a strong entry. 6484, and flushes/recoveries of 6447 are of interest below there.

2928: 
2929: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from yesterday’s 6593 Failed Breakdown that occurred at 11am Tuesday and this evolved into a staggering 250+ point long and this is exactly why I hold runners. We had another massive run today so readers know what I’m going to say here. My job as a Failed Breakdown trader is to wait for those scary elevator down sells where people are panicking, calling tops, chasing short. Then, I long when a low/shelf of lows recovers. Then, we rip, and I hold runners. After we rip - my work is done. I simply don’t trade until we get another Failed Breakdown. I don’t chase, don’t force trades, don’t trade chop. As readers know, after a major structural breakout (like we saw of the big white flag from February shown in my below chart that occurred Tuesday) setups often become scarce. This is fine - it’s what runners are for. Price tends to give tons and tons of opportunities to get in prior to these moves, but once it breaks, it breaks. Today, ES broke out another small flag which we built from Tuesday’s high into this morning. Generally resistance of this is 6821-26 now (this was the region we lifted off from this morning) and bulls want to hold it or quick trap below on any dips tomorrow. As of writing though, 6848 is first support down. At 2:35PM today, we set a nice 18 point low there. I would not buy 6848 directly now, but if we can flush that low and recover it would be a lower quality Failed Breakdown. Below 6848 ES likely flushes down to 6821-26. While one could just give this a bid, remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed in ES. If we are plunging full speed on some headline, never get infront. If we are knifing at full speed the much safer entry is to wait for a Failed Breakdown. In this case there are no significant lows nearby, but if we can flush and then recover 6826 this may be an entry. 6809 is below there and this is also a decent support. Below there is the first possible Failed Breakdown. Specifically, at ~10:30AM today, ES set a clear significant low at 6802 from which we rallied to today’s highs. The flush of this low and recovery is a quality Failed Breakdown (ideal if it tags 6793 in the process). 6780 is below there. This was yesterday’s (Wednesday’s) low of day. A flush and recovery of this low is a clear Failed Breakdown and a powerful one. Below there 6756 is a support to watch.

Typically I always use it when there is not clear, obvious, “jumps out at you” acceptance.

3097: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 9:28AM 6525 Failed Breakdown we saw today, discussed above. Also note that markets are closed tomorrow for Good Friday, meaning this plan is for Monday. We had a massive rip today in ES but we ultimately closed not far from where we did yesterday. Bulls did what they had to do though - they bought the dip. On Monday evening at 830PM, ES put in a massive Failed Breakdown where we swept the Sunday/Monday regular hours shelf of lows at 6360 down to 6353. We then rallied ~290 points into this weeks highs. After a big squeeze like this, a dip is inevitable, but the question is whether or not that dip gets bought on the first pullback. Today it did, we sold down to 6506, recovered a big low at 6520 set at 2am around 9:30AM, and ripped. With the dip bought, the task for bulls now is to keep the momentum going and we will hold runners. As of writing 6604 is first support down. This is a sloppy, messy zone now and not one I would engage. If we can flush it down to 6592 and recover though. This is actionable. 6592 remains an extremely important support line in its own right. It was support multiple times on Wednesday, failed last evening, then has been support today since noon. Instead of just buying it, the Failed Breakdown of 6592 is actionable. Note to be precise, there is 12:20PM low at 6586 and a 2pm low at 6593. Ideally you want both these flushed and recovered. If 6592 fails its back down the levels and there is not much until 6553. If its a controlled sell into this region, one can bid it. But remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed in ES. If we are plunging full speed on some headline, never get infront. The safer alternative is to wait for 6553 to defend and recover the first level up which is 6563. Below 6553 we taken another dive. 6525-30 is a weaker support but its a high risk long. The strongest support is 6499. While one could bid this, a much clearer entry is the Failed Breakdown of today’s daily low which is located at 6505. If ES can flush 6505 down to 6499 and recover 6505, this is a strong entry. 6484, and flushes/recoveries of 6447 are of interest below there.

1802: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my my 10% long runner from the 6793 Failed Breakdown at 8:45PM Sunday April 12th in the evening. My most recent entries wwere the 1:50PM Thursday Failed Breakdown of 7085 and the 3:55PM Thursday Failed Breakdown of 7129. We are closing at the highs today and readers know what I’m going to say here. There is nothing to do now but simply hold my runner and wait for the next elevator down sell. My job is to trade when we are free falling, influencer accounts are posting the top is in, and everyone is bearish. Then I follow institutions in when they trap those shorts with a Failed Breakdown. We then rip. After this, there is nothing for me to do. We had an extremely nice rally from yesterday’s 1:50PM Failed Breakdown of 7085 and I would strongly encourage studying this deeply if you are knew to Failed Breakdowns as this was textbook and had all the components you want to see: 1) An elevator down sell 2) A clear shelf of daily lows at 7085 3) A sweep of that shelf. Of course only a tiny minority of Failed breakdowns are this perfect, but it is a good starting place. I would also strongly encourage reading my paragraph on Mode 2 price action at the beginning of the Trade Recap section above as Failed Breakdowns of this caliber are most common in Mode 2 ranges. Not only are they common, they are a defining feature of Mode 2 ranges. Once a range is established (the last week its been mostly 7085 to 7180 as discussed above) price loves to trap below the range, then rip. It often does this multiple times. It also does the same on the upside on occasion (failed breakouts - which I do not trade in ES as the win rate is too low). Heading into Monday, this range is still in play. Resistance is generally 7180 (trendline connecting the last Friday, this Tuesday, and this Thursday lower highs). Support is still basically 7085. We are probing outside the range into the close today but remember we don’t predict. We don’t say “this is breaking out” or “this will be a trap”. We don’t care, we can’t know unless you are a psychic, and it is irrelevant since a Failed Breakdown isn’t present. We wait on our triggers, react, hold runner. Don’t predict - ever. First support down is 7180. This is very close to the highs and I have no interest in engaging this. A very high risk entry for those wanting to trade though would be if we can roughly defend 7180, then recover 7185. Between noon and 2:30PM today, ES set a shelf of lows at 7185. If we can flush to 7180 and recover that shelf, it is a high risk trade. Not much of interest below there until 7147 and this is where we get into better quality Failed Breakdowns. Specifically at ~9:40AM today ES set a big low at ~7147. It was from this that ES rallied. A Failed Breakdown of this low is an actionable long. Bonus if this tags 7135. Below there is 7121. Instead of bidding this directly, a safer entry is to wait for a Failed Breakdown of 7121. At 3:45PM yesterday ES set a massive low at 7121 and the flush and recover here is actionable. Below there ES probably just free falls down to range support. A very clear setup here is the Failed Breakdown of yesterday’s 7080 daily low. If ES can sweep this down to 7074 and recover 7080, this is a high quality long. Below there, watch for Failed Breakdowns of 7058.

2929: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from yesterday’s 6593 Failed Breakdown that occurred at 11am Tuesday and this evolved into a staggering 250+ point long and this is exactly why I hold runners. We had another massive run today so readers know what I’m going to say here. My job as a Failed Breakdown trader is to wait for those scary elevator down sells where people are panicking, calling tops, chasing short. Then, I long when a low/shelf of lows recovers. Then, we rip, and I hold runners. After we rip - my work is done. I simply don’t trade until we get another Failed Breakdown. I don’t chase, don’t force trades, don’t trade chop. As readers know, after a major structural breakout (like we saw of the big white flag from February shown in my below chart that occurred Tuesday) setups often become scarce. This is fine - it’s what runners are for. Price tends to give tons and tons of opportunities to get in prior to these moves, but once it breaks, it breaks. Today, ES broke out another small flag which we built from Tuesday’s high into this morning. Generally resistance of this is 6821-26 now (this was the region we lifted off from this morning) and bulls want to hold it or quick trap below on any dips tomorrow. As of writing though, 6848 is first support down. At 2:35PM today, we set a nice 18 point low there. I would not buy 6848 directly now, but if we can flush that low and recover it would be a lower quality Failed Breakdown. Below 6848 ES likely flushes down to 6821-26. While one could just give this a bid, remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed in ES. If we are plunging full speed on some headline, never get infront. If we are knifing at full speed the much safer entry is to wait for a Failed Breakdown. In this case there are no significant lows nearby, but if we can flush and then recover 6826 this may be an entry. 6809 is below there and this is also a decent support. Below there is the first possible Failed Breakdown. Specifically, at ~10:30AM today, ES set a clear significant low at 6802 from which we rallied to today’s highs. The flush of this low and recovery is a quality Failed Breakdown (ideal if it tags 6793 in the process). 6780 is below there. This was yesterday’s (Wednesday’s) low of day. A flush and recovery of this low is a clear Failed Breakdown and a powerful one. Below there 6756 is a support to watch.

2418: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my my 10% long runner from the 6793 Failed Breakdown at 8:45PM Sunday evening. My most recent entry was the 10:45AM 7056 low quality Failed Breakdown yesterday. This has been a staggering 315 point runner this week and one of the best this year. Despite this though readers know that I am a long only trader in ES. I do not short, ever regardless if it is the strongest downtrend in years. Some would then think that moves like the last week are a therefore a dream for me to trade. They would be wrong though. As I’ve said many times, even though I only trade Failed Breakdowns, I infinitely prefer trading volatile, downrending markets. Why? There are more setups. On an average reddish day, ES will go elevator down, put in a Failed Breakdown, then rip 30 or 40 or 70 points. Then it will collapse again, put in another Failed Breakdown, then maybe produce another 10 or 20 point bounce. Then it will collapse again, and often then bottom out (usually late day), put in a Failed Breakdown, and log in a big green day the next day. On this day, there would be three big trades. This week, there has been maybe 2-3 setups all week. Price just grinds up with minimal entries - you would need to chase to get in and chasing can work in unusual contexts but on balance it is doesn’t. On a red week, we get 2-3 setups daily, and some days (if its a really volatile day) its not uncommon to get a setup per hour or even more. Many new traders just don’t get this. They think red markets means you should short and green means you should long. Its more like if there is volatility, you will get paid regardless whether your strat is long or short only. Weeks like this are what runners are for though. Yet again today though we closed up near the highs and way up here, there is simply nothing to do. . As a Failed Breakdown trader, I trade in one condition only: After a scary elevator down sell that then produces a Failed Breakdown. After that, I enter, and we rip. Then there is nothing to do until the next one other than ride runner. Don’t make trading more complex than this. It will take a deep sell to get me interested again. To make matters worse, I find that often, but not always, the first proper red day after a big rally can be crushing for longs. This is because many eager chasers will want to “buy the dip”, expecting an instant recovery. Instead price just sells all day. Longs trigger, run a level or two, fail, and repeat. This is fine for us level to level traders, but be warned. 7153 is first support down and ES built a shelf of lows here since 1:20PM. I’m just not interested in engaging a support this close to the highs because when we get a red day (and we will) first supports will get crushed. A possible entry though would be if would flush that 7153 shelf (perhaps down to 7147) and then recover 7153. Below 7153 is 7120. Again, not interested in buying this - however - at 9:45AM today ES set a massive low there from which ES rallied 67 points to the high of day. A failed breakdown of this low would be actionable for a level to level move. 7104 is below there. I won’t buy this either but for those interesting in engaging one could try a bid here but just remember my core rule: No knife catching allowed in ES. If we are plunging full speed on some headline, never get infront. If we are knifing at full speed the much safer entry is to wait for a Failed Breakdown. If its a slow controlled sell into the zone one can buy it but if we are knifing at least wait for it it to flush and recover. 7085 is below there and this backtests the shelf we built from noon yesterday until 7am this morning. Finally, if we get a proper red day on Monday, watch out for Failed Breakdowns of 7058. We set a big low here at 220pm yesterday and the Failed Breakdown is actionable.

158: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 12:20PM 7213 Failed breakdown on Monday. My most recent entry was the 7255 Failed Breakdown at noon today. Today we finally got some red and as a result we saw setups for the first time since Monday. I said this yesterday, and it was applicable today: “Readers know I always say that while I only trade the long side, I infinitely prefer either red or sideways markets to rising markets. Why? Because as a day trader, I get paid on volatility, not direction. Red and sideways markets are more volatile. More volatile means more Failed Breakdowns, and larger squeezes. On a very green day or week, there may be 2 or setups all week. On a red day (like Monday) we sometimes get 3 in a day, often more. Yes on a real red day (which we inevitably get soon), those Failed Breakdowns may only pop 10, 20, 50 points before rejecting down. This is fine - we got paid, and usually there are several opportunities to do so on a good red day.” After this weeks parabolic rally ES is likely into a “rest and digest” phase now and hopefully this means more two way action than we’ve seen the last week as ES builds a new consolidation or corrects more. As always though - we don’t predict. We just plan zones and react. One speculative range for this new consolidation could be 7402 to 7340-45, but this is very early and the downside support of this range could easily end up far lower. 7340-45 is first support down. We’ve already engaged this extensively holding it at 1:20PM and 2:40PM, forming a shelf. The Failed Breakdown of this shelf is an actionable long. 7327 is below there. This is a very obvious support shelf from Monday that we defended 2x on Monday morning before rallying to the weekly highs and the flush and recovery of this shelf is an actionable Failed Breakdown. Nothing below until 7311. This is a good support. If it is a slower grind down into this zone, one can try the bid there. If we are knifing into it full speed though, no knife catching allowed. If we are knifing full power, a safer option is the Failed Breakdown of 7306. From midnight until 420AM Wednesday ES set a nice shelf of lows at 7306 and sweeps and recoveries of this shelf are actionable. 7279 is below there but if we are knifing full power don’t just instantly buy this - wait for it to hold then recover 7283 to get long. Nothing below there until 7257.

As I’ve said many times, even though I only trade Failed Breakdowns, I infinitely prefer trading volatile, downrending markets. Why? There are more setups. On an average reddish day, ES will go elevator down, put in a Failed Breakdown, then rip 30 or 40 or 70 points. Then it will collapse again, put in another Failed Breakdown, then maybe produce another 10 or 20 point bounce. Then it will collapse again, and often then bottom out (usually late day), put in a Failed Breakdown, and log in a big green day the next day. On this day, there would be three big trades. This week, there has been maybe 2-3 setups all week. Price just grinds up with minimal entries - you would need to chase to get in and chasing can work in unusual contexts but on balance it is doesn’t. On a red week, we get 2-3 setups daily, and some days (if its a really volatile day) its not uncommon to get a setup per hour or even more. Many new traders just don’t get this. They think red markets means you should short and green means you should long. Its more like if there is volatility, you will get paid regardless whether your strat is long or short only. Weeks like this are what runners are for though. Yet again today though we closed up near the highs and way up here, there is simply nothing to do. . As a Failed Breakdown trader, I trade in one condition only: After a scary elevator down sell that then produces a Failed Breakdown. After that, I enter, and we rip. Then there is nothing to do until the next one other than ride runner. Don’t make trading more complex than this. It will take a deep sell to get me interested again. To make matters worse, I find that often, but not always, the first proper red day after a big rally can be crushing for longs. This is because many eager chasers will want to “buy the dip”, expecting an instant recovery. Instead price just sells all day. Longs trigger, run a level or two, fail, and repeat. This is fine for us level to level traders, but be warned. 7153 is first support down and ES built a shelf of lows here since 1:20PM. I’m just not interested in engaging a support this close to the highs because when we get a red day (and we will) first supports will get crushed. A possible entry though would be if would flush that 7153 shelf (perhaps down to 7147) and then recover 7153. Below 7153 is 7120. Again, not interested in buying this - however - at 9:45AM today ES set a massive low there from which ES rallied 67 points to the high of day. A failed breakdown of this low would be actionable for a level to level move. 7104 is below there. I won’t buy this either but for those interesting in engaging one could try a bid here but just remember my core rule: No knife catching allowed in ES. If we are plunging full speed on some headline, never get infront. If we are knifing at full speed the much safer entry is to wait for a Failed Breakdown. If its a slow controlled sell into the zone one can buy it but if we are knifing at least wait for it it to flush and recover. 7085 is below there and this backtests the shelf we built from noon yesterday until 7am this morning. Finally, if we get a proper red day on Monday, watch out for Failed Breakdowns of 7058. We set a big low here at 220pm yesterday and the Failed Breakdown is actionable.

1952: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my my 10% long runner from the 6793 Failed Breakdown at 8:45PM Sunday April 12th in the evening. My most recent entry was the 4:07PM 7097 Failed Breakdown yesterday, discussed above. Heading into tomorrow, little has changed in ES. We remain inside a clear range which has been building since Friday. Resistance is roughly 7180 now (trendline connecting the Friday and Tuesday highs). Support is now roughly 7085. This is where we set the Sunday low of day, and yesterday’s low of day. 7147 and 7111 will be/are key pivots inside this range and everything in this range = chop. Ranges like this are what I call Mode 2. Mode 2 is easy to trade as long as you follow the rules. 1) Do not predict price action. Do not predict what path price will take to fill out the range, when it will breakout, or how it will breakout. If you try to predict, you’ve lost already. Simply plan triggers, react. 2) Always manage trades level to level. In ranges price typically does not follow through cleanly all day (these are Mode 1 days and are only 10% of overall sessions). 3) Trade the traps. Mode 2 ranges love to put in Failed Breakdowns. We saw one after 4pm yesterday. Price will flush range support (or a low inside the range), trap, reverse. We simply wait on those. Finally, when its ready, the range will breakout. About 80% of ranges resolve in the direction of the trend, but we do not predict this. We just plan our triggers, and react. First support down is 7147 now. We already engaged this extensively today, defending it at 12:50PM and bouncing 15 points. I would not buy 7147 again, but if we can flush that 12:50PM low set there and recover, this is a low quality Failed Breakdown. I say low quality because it falls a little shy of the 20 point low threshold I like to see, but it is all we have here. Below 7147 is 7129. Instead of just buying this, wait for a Failed breakdown. At 1:10AM yesterday we set a huge low here from which we rallied into today’s highs and the flush and recovery of this low is actionable. 7111-08 is below there. I won’t buy this, but if its a slow grind into this zone one can try a bid there. Just remember no knife catching allowed in ES. If we are knifing full speed - do not buy this. 7085 is below there and as stated this was a massive low of day yesterday and Sunday. A Failed Breakdown of this low is a high quality trade. 7058 is below there and watch for Failed Breakdowns of that low.

My most recent entry was the 4:20PM 7137 Failed breakdown this Wednesday, provided in advance and reviewed in Wednesday’s newsletter. This week was a testament to why runners are so important in ES. When I got long at 4:20PM Wednesday after the major Failed Breakdown post-earnings of 7137, did I know we were about to go 150 points straight up? I am always bullish in an uptrend, but nobody ever knows the exact path higher price will take. Fortunately we don’t need to. We just manage level to level, let the runner work. The main benefit of runners is they control FOMO. When you have a runner, you don’t feel the urge to chase, or the urge to rush in. You have exposure. Price keeps going up? Great - runner works. Price goes elevator down - great, we get another entry. Unfortunately though, readers also know what I’m going to say here. We had yet another monster run today and while we did cool off from the highs, there are just no real quality, confident entries until we get a proper elevator down sell. 7264 is 1st support down. This has been support all afternoon. I would not buy this again, but at 240PM we set a low at 7259. Sweeps of this low and recoveries are a possible low quality Failed Breakdown. I am always cautious the day after a large squeeze for two reasons. Both pertain to the fact that price needs to digest a large move like this. This can occur in two ways: 1) A red day. If this occurs, every nearby support will fail after perhaps a quick scalp. 2) Price could build out a consolidation (this will probably be in a range between 7248 and 7300). This is fine for us day traders since we take profits level to level. Early on the digestion process, we don’t know which it will be which is why I take things very light. 7248 is below there. There is a nice shelf at 7248 and at 5am we set a big low there from which we rallied 50+ points. The Failed Breakdown of this is actionable. If that fails we probably start a nice retracement finally. Watch for Failed Breakdowns of 7237 (take profits fast on this one) and not much under there until 7212. If its a slow, controlled grind into 7212 one can try a bid here but remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed. If we are selling vertically you never long. 7198 is below there, this is also a decent support and it roughly backtests the week long range we broke out Thursday. If we are full speed knifing down, the safe entry here is to wait for price to flush through that, and also flush 7193. At 1240pm Thursday ES set a clear major low here and if you wait for that low to sweep and recover its much safer than just buying 7198 and hoping for the best. Nothing under there until 7160 and Failed breakdowns of 7160 are an excellent long. There is a very nice two touch set of lows at 7157-61. There is the 520AM low at 7167 from which we ripped to the morning highs Thursday, then there is the 7157 low from which we ripped to the current highs.

I very frequently talk about how even though I only trade long as a Failed Breakdown trader, I prefer red days. Why? Red days have volatility. They have elevator down sells. Once we get a big Failed Breakdown though (like yesterday of the 7213 6:20AM Monday low) and it sticks, we are without setups until the next selloff and we can just hold runners as price grinds higher slowly. 2050: As I emphasize daily, my “job description” is to wait patiently for ES to get injected with volatility and put in a strong elevator down sell, trap shorts, then put in a Failed Breakdown or Reclaim. Then I engage. After price has done that - or if price is not doing that - I literally do not care what price is doing, I just hold my runner. This is what trading with an edge means. Price ripping? Don’t care. Price selling 200 points? Don’t care. I just care about sells insofar as they are a precondition to setup a Level Reclaim or Failed Breakdown for me.

795: 
796: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 1:50PM last Thursday Failed Breakdown of 7085 - discussed extensively in Thursday’s newsletter and provided in advance. My most recent entry was the 4:20PM 7137 Failed breakdown this Wednesday, provided in advance and reviewed in Wednesday’s newsletter. This week was a testament to why runners are so important in ES. When I got long at 4:20PM Wednesday after the major Failed Breakdown post-earnings of 7137, did I know we were about to go 150 points straight up? I am always bullish in an uptrend, but nobody ever knows the exact path higher price will take. Fortunately we don’t need to. We just manage level to level, let the runner work. The main benefit of runners is they control FOMO. When you have a runner, you don’t feel the urge to chase, or the urge to rush in. You have exposure. Price keeps going up? Great - runner works. Price goes elevator down - great, we get another entry. Unfortunately though, readers also know what I’m going to say here. We had yet another monster run today and while we did cool off from the highs, there are just no real quality, confident entries until we get a proper elevator down sell. 7264 is 1st support down. This has been support all afternoon. I would not buy this again, but at 240PM we set a low at 7259. Sweeps of this low and recoveries are a possible low quality Failed Breakdown. I am always cautious the day after a large squeeze for two reasons. Both pertain to the fact that price needs to digest a large move like this. This can occur in two ways: 1) A red day. If this occurs, every nearby support will fail after perhaps a quick scalp. 2) Price could build out a consolidation (this will probably be in a range between 7248 and 7300). This is fine for us day traders since we take profits level to level. Early on the digestion process, we don’t know which it will be which is why I take things very light. 7248 is below there. There is a nice shelf at 7248 and at 5am we set a big low there from which we rallied 50+ points. The Failed Breakdown of this is actionable. If that fails we probably start a nice retracement finally. Watch for Failed Breakdowns of 7237 (take profits fast on this one) and not much under there until 7212. If its a slow, controlled grind into 7212 one can try a bid here but remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed. If we are selling vertically you never long. 7198 is below there, this is also a decent support and it roughly backtests the week long range we broke out Thursday. If we are full speed knifing down, the safe entry here is to wait for price to flush through that, and also flush 7193. At 1240pm Thursday ES set a clear major low here and if you wait for that low to sweep and recover its much safer than just buying 7198 and hoping for the best. Nothing under there until 7160 and Failed breakdowns of 7160 are an excellent long. There is a very nice two touch set of lows at 7157-61. There is the 520AM low at 7167 from which we ripped to the morning highs Thursday, then there is the 7157 low from which we ripped to the current highs.

1952: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my my 10% long runner from the 6793 Failed Breakdown at 8:45PM Sunday April 12th in the evening. My most recent entry was the 4:07PM 7097 Failed Breakdown yesterday, discussed above. Heading into tomorrow, little has changed in ES. We remain inside a clear range which has been building since Friday. Resistance is roughly 7180 now (trendline connecting the Friday and Tuesday highs). Support is now roughly 7085. This is where we set the Sunday low of day, and yesterday’s low of day. 7147 and 7111 will be/are key pivots inside this range and everything in this range = chop. Ranges like this are what I call Mode 2. Mode 2 is easy to trade as long as you follow the rules. 1) Do not predict price action. Do not predict what path price will take to fill out the range, when it will breakout, or how it will breakout. If you try to predict, you’ve lost already. Simply plan triggers, react. 2) Always manage trades level to level. In ranges price typically does not follow through cleanly all day (these are Mode 1 days and are only 10% of overall sessions). 3) Trade the traps. Mode 2 ranges love to put in Failed Breakdowns. We saw one after 4pm yesterday. Price will flush range support (or a low inside the range), trap, reverse. We simply wait on those. Finally, when its ready, the range will breakout. About 80% of ranges resolve in the direction of the trend, but we do not predict this. We just plan our triggers, and react. First support down is 7147 now. We already engaged this extensively today, defending it at 12:50PM and bouncing 15 points. I would not buy 7147 again, but if we can flush that 12:50PM low set there and recover, this is a low quality Failed Breakdown. I say low quality because it falls a little shy of the 20 point low threshold I like to see, but it is all we have here. Below 7147 is 7129. Instead of just buying this, wait for a Failed breakdown. At 1:10AM yesterday we set a huge low here from which we rallied into today’s highs and the flush and recovery of this low is actionable. 7111-08 is below there. I won’t buy this, but if its a slow grind into this zone one can try a bid there. Just remember no knife catching allowed in ES. If we are knifing full speed - do not buy this. 7085 is below there and as stated this was a massive low of day yesterday and Sunday. A Failed Breakdown of this low is a high quality trade. 7058 is below there and watch for Failed Breakdowns of that low.

raw_mancini_source.quote: After spending Friday and last week rallying almost non-stop, ES is finally back into consolidation mode and has spent all week building a new, large, constantly morphing consolidation. This will (like all ranges) constantly morph but as of now is something like 7100-05 to 7180 with 7120 being a major magnet in the middle of the range, as well as 7147. As always - the cardinal rule of day trading is we never predict. We don’t predict how the range will expand, which way it will break, or when. We just plan our zones, react when price gets to them, then manage level to level. The second you predict, you’ve already lost. Price will follow highly complex pathways within this range and will never conform to what you predict, so don’t try…..If ES does sell tomorrow, bulls just want see a quick flush of today’s 7097 daily low then recovery. When this range is ready, next leg up targets 7202, 7220, the 7235. There is nowhere to add on strength that isn’t highly complex here, but one can try the 7147 reclaim. Be sure to see acceptance first.

raw_mancini_source.quote: From my perspective - we were at 7130’s heading into the close yesterday and there was little to do but wait for an elevator down sell and Failed Breakdown. As I emphasize daily, my “job description” is to wait patiently for ES to get injected with volatility and put in a strong elevator down sell, trap shorts, then put in a Failed Breakdown or Reclaim. Then I engage. After price has done that - or if price is not doing that - I literally do not care what price is doing, I just hold my runner. This is what trading with an edge means. Price ripping? Don’t care. Price selling 200 points? Don’t care. I just care about sells insofar as they are a precondition to setup a Level Reclaim or Failed Breakdown for me.

My most recent entry was the 840PM 7185 Failed Breakdown last evening, discussed above. Today was a slow paced consolidation session or Mode 2 day as I frequently discuss but we are yet again closing at the high of day. This means there is nothing for me to do. As I say over and over my job as a Failed Breakdown trader is to wait for an elevator down sell, wait for lows to sweep, then I long, then we rip. After the rip starts there is nothing to do but hold runner until the next one. In addition, please read the excerpt at the beginning of the trade recap section above. As I frequently say, about 90% of days in ES are Mode 2 days and Mode 2 refers to sessions where price is rangebound, does not trend cleanly one way from open to close, and puts in failed breakdowns at the support of the range. For most of today, this range was a tight base with 7175-80 support, and 7200-05 resistance. Everything in this=tight chop. Ultimately, this week is all about Wednesday where we have FOMC and most major tech companies reporting earnings. As always - we never predict. We don’t predict what way a range will break, what path it will take. We simply defer to the trend, and we respond to our triggers - that is it. First support down is 7198. There is not much here I’d be interested in engaging as there are no significant lows at this level. If we flush that zone and recover there is a desperation long here for anyone not already in but I’ll be passing on this. Next down is 7175-80 and this is where I get a little more interested with this zone being range support. There was a clear shelf of lows at 7180. At 4:20AM Monday and 9:45AM Monday ES set 18 point lows there on each respective bounce. Then at 11:25AM today we swept that shelf to 7177 and set another low. If ES can put in a Failed Breakdown of this entire structure (perhaps down to 7161) this would be a decent Failed BReakdown. Nothing below there until 7149. This is a decent support. If its a controlled grind into the zone one can try a bid here, but if we are knifing full speed there is a much safer Failed Breakdown available. Specifically, at 940AM on Friday ES set a massive low at ~7149 from which we rallied to the current highs. A Failed Breakdown of this low is an obvious entry (bonus if we tag 7135 on this). Below there watch traps of 7121 and if we really flush tomorrow, an obvious trade would be the Failed Breakdown of Thursday’s 7080 daily low. This is very high quality.

Readers know I always say that while I only trade the long side, I infinitely prefer either red or sideways markets to rising markets. Why? Because as a day trader, I get paid on volatility, not direction. Red and sideways markets are more volatile. More volatile means more Failed Breakdowns, and larger squeezes. On a very green day or week, there may be 2 or setups all week. On a red day (like Monday) we sometimes get 3 in a day, often more. Yes on a real red day (which we inevitably get soon), those Failed Breakdowns may only pop 10, 20, 50 points before rejecting down. This is fine - we got paid, and usually there are several opportunities to do so on a good red day. Up here though, there is simply nothing to do but wait for a deep sell. 7369 is 1st support down. I am never overly interested in buying the first support down after a rally - it will only hold if the parabola continues, and if we get a red day (or even a half red day) the first several supports will get crushed by definition. If we can wick below 7369 and recover its a possible desperation long but not one I’d engage. Read the reaction there. 7345 is below there. This is also a shallow support. If its a slow grind into the zone one can buy it, but if we are free falling, don’t knife catch. A much safer entry is to wait for 7345 to defend, then recover 7355 to get long. Why 7355? At 12:30PM today we set a big low there and ripped into the high of day. This is a clear significant low and the flush and recovery of that is actionable. 7332 is below there. Watch for sweeps and recoveries (Failed Breakdowns) of this as well. Between 8am and 930am we set a shelf here. Some wicks on this technically went down to 7327 so you want to see that whole shelf sweep then recover 7332. 7311 is below there - this is a strong support but only bid if it we are not free falling full speed. Below there watch for Failed Breakdowns of 7284.

1644: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 1:50PM Thursday Failed Breakdown of 7085 - discussed extensively in Thursday’s newsletter and provided in advance. My most recent entry was the 840PM 7185 Failed Breakdown last evening, discussed above. Today was a slow paced consolidation session or Mode 2 day as I frequently discuss but we are yet again closing at the high of day. This means there is nothing for me to do. As I say over and over my job as a Failed Breakdown trader is to wait for an elevator down sell, wait for lows to sweep, then I long, then we rip. After the rip starts there is nothing to do but hold runner until the next one. In addition, please read the excerpt at the beginning of the trade recap section above. As I frequently say, about 90% of days in ES are Mode 2 days and Mode 2 refers to sessions where price is rangebound, does not trend cleanly one way from open to close, and puts in failed breakdowns at the support of the range. For most of today, this range was a tight base with 7175-80 support, and 7200-05 resistance. Everything in this=tight chop. Ultimately, this week is all about Wednesday where we have FOMC and most major tech companies reporting earnings. As always - we never predict. We don’t predict what way a range will break, what path it will take. We simply defer to the trend, and we respond to our triggers - that is it. First support down is 7198. There is not much here I’d be interested in engaging as there are no significant lows at this level. If we flush that zone and recover there is a desperation long here for anyone not already in but I’ll be passing on this. Next down is 7175-80 and this is where I get a little more interested with this zone being range support. There was a clear shelf of lows at 7180. At 4:20AM Monday and 9:45AM Monday ES set 18 point lows there on each respective bounce. Then at 11:25AM today we swept that shelf to 7177 and set another low. If ES can put in a Failed Breakdown of this entire structure (perhaps down to 7161) this would be a decent Failed BReakdown. Nothing below there until 7149. This is a decent support. If its a controlled grind into the zone one can try a bid here, but if we are knifing full speed there is a much safer Failed Breakdown available. Specifically, at 940AM on Friday ES set a massive low at ~7149 from which we rallied to the current highs. A Failed Breakdown of this low is an obvious entry (bonus if we tag 7135 on this). Below there watch traps of 7121 and if we really flush tomorrow, an obvious trade would be the Failed Breakdown of Thursday’s 7080 daily low. This is very high quality.
