We had an incredible rip today and unfortunately this means we returned to the same low volatility grind up. I very frequently talk about how even though I only trade long as a Failed Breakdown trader, I prefer red days. Why? Red days have volatility. They have elevator down sells. And when there are elevator down sells, there are Failed Breakdowns, and short squeezes of varying sizes that we can capture points on. Once we get a big Failed Breakdown though (like yesterday of the 7213 6:20AM Monday low) and it sticks, we are without setups until the next selloff and we can just hold runners as price grinds higher slowly. That is the position we are into the close today and with ES closing near the highs, there is nothing to do again until we get the next flush. As of writing first support down is 7289. I have no interest in bidding the first support down after a big rally like this. 7279 is below there. Between 11:30AM and 1:10PM today, ES set a nice multi-touch shelf of lows at 7279. This is a 5-6 touch shelf. The flush and recovery of this is actionable as a Failed Breakdown, but given its position in the trend leg from yesterday (top quarter) this is riskier than others because its success would require a continued parabolic rally. Not much below there until 7258. If price is grinding slowly into this level, one can try a bid there, but remember my core rule in ES. No knife catching allowed. If we are knifing full speed into it on some big headline - always wait for a Failed Breakdown. In this case, the most obvious nearby Failed Breakdown is a little below 7258. Specifically, at 7:10AM today at 7248 ES set a clear, obvious low from which we rallied to the daily highs. The Failed Breakdown of this is actionable. We should flush quite hard below there. 7213 is one support of interest as long as we are grinding down in a controlled manner (not I ONLY trade Failed Breakdowns so these direct bids are not ones I would take). Then below there, the Failed Breakdown of yesterday’s 7199 daily low is high quality.

Resistance has been up at 7189 for the last 24hrs, with 7174 being a key pivot inside this range. 7147 is first support down of interest. This level is totally washed out now and I would never buy it. The only setup is interest is the Failed Breakdown of today’s low which is at 7137. If we can trap this and recover, one can try the long. If you want to be extra safe here, wait for 7147 to recover to long. If you don’t you need to lock in partial gains at 7147 since it will be resistance. Below 7135 is 7121. We set a nice low there at 3:50PM last Thursday and the Failed Breakdown of that low is actionable. We could freefall below there. Nothing below there until 7105. On a normal day, this is a decent support. However, if we are knifing down on earnings do not buy this. My hardest rule in ES is no knife catching allowed. When a sell algo is on and activated by a headline - no support matters. Only engage this level if price tests it in a slow, controlled sell that is not headline driven. Not much below there until 7078. Not much below there until 7078. Last Thursday, we set a major low of day here. The Failed Breakdown of this low is obviously actionable. If we can flush it and tag 7068, this is a huge bonus. Below there watch for Failed Breakdowns of 7048 (we set a nice low there at 10am last Thursday). 7016-20 is a strong support under there that one can engage as long as we are not going elevator down.

1767: Remember the non-acceptance protocol automatically activates when price recovers the significant low (7125) by 5 points (7130) and holds at or above 7130 for a few minutes. The non-acceptance protocol typically triggers for shallow (under 10 point flush of the significant low) and fast Failed Breakdowns, but you can use it for any Failed Breakdown. Typically I always use it when there is not clear, obvious, “jumps out at you” acceptance.

1258: I need to see a vertical “elevator down” sell. Then I long when price flushes a low/shelf of lows for a Failed Breakdown. That is it. I don’t short, I don’t chase longs, I don’t flip back and forth long and short, I don’t trade chop, I don’t trade low vol, I don’t trade because some influencer posted a trade, I don’t trade because of news. I don’t care if price sells 500 points or 100 points - nothing to do with me. I don’t care what price is doing unless it is putting in an elevator down sell into a Failed Breakdown. This is day trading is. 99.9% of what you see on social media is just people gambling or engagement farming.

raw_mancini_source.quote: Bull case tomorrow: ES is now in the process of building a highly complex consolidation since April 17. This structure was mostly 7080 support with 7180 resistance. On Friday we broke this structure out. Then all day Monday, 7180 flipped to support, and we ripped to ~7225. Today, we then lost 7180 and it flipped back to resistance much of the session. The bull case tomorrow is that (in the most extreme form) bulls want to hold 7147 support (or quick trap below). From there, bulls want to reclaim 7180. Targets then become 7186, 7204, 7214, then 7234, then 7246. Given tomorrows catalysts, price is very unlikely to follow a clean path and will rather trap over and over again - again if have not mastered not predicting price, take tomorrow off. Once the session starts, enter pure reaction mode and do not predict anything. In terms of spots to add on strength, 7180 reclaims are a possible entry. We have extensively accepted it today with one backtest at 950AM and another at around 3pm.

1205: 2) The basic theme heading into today was that ES spent all last week resting and consolidating after a massive 100+ point rally last Friday. Generally this consolidation formed a large range with 7080 support (this was the Sunday low and Tuesday low last week) and 7180 resistance (this was a downsloping trendline connecting the last Friday high, last Tuesday high, and last Thursday high). As stated above last Thursday ES swept support of that range, trapped bears, then ripped the other direction back up the range to mildly break out on Friday. On Monday, resistance of this range at 7180 flipped to support, then we ran to 7225. Yesterday, we failed back into the range, and 7180 acted as resistance all day. Heading into today, I discussed at 4pm yesterday what bulls need to see:

1208: 
1209: 3) Finally, while I discuss plenty of structures and levels above, its vital that we do not use any of the above to make predictions. Our job as traders is never to predict what price will do (literally impossible on an intraday basis). Our job is to plan zones, wait for price to present our setups at those zones, then manage the trade systematically level to level. I am not exagerating at all when I say the core difference between winning and losing traders is that losing traders are obssessed with knowing where price will go. Professionals don’t care - they let price pick the path and react. This allows one to trade what they see, not what they think, and not be affected by bias. When you predict, you are giving yourself a bias which blinds you from objectively trading the action. Price traps by its nature and takes the pathway engineered to cause the maximum pain to retail traders. You can pretend that it is a supercomputer that is intentionally trying to ensure that no retail traders profit from any large move, and ensure they are all stopped out, trapped, and frustrated before one occurs.

157: 
158: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 12:20PM 7213 Failed breakdown on Monday. My most recent entry was the 7255 Failed Breakdown at noon today. Today we finally got some red and as a result we saw setups for the first time since Monday. I said this yesterday, and it was applicable today: “Readers know I always say that while I only trade the long side, I infinitely prefer either red or sideways markets to rising markets. Why? Because as a day trader, I get paid on volatility, not direction. Red and sideways markets are more volatile. More volatile means more Failed Breakdowns, and larger squeezes. On a very green day or week, there may be 2 or setups all week. On a red day (like Monday) we sometimes get 3 in a day, often more. Yes on a real red day (which we inevitably get soon), those Failed Breakdowns may only pop 10, 20, 50 points before rejecting down. This is fine - we got paid, and usually there are several opportunities to do so on a good red day.” After this weeks parabolic rally ES is likely into a “rest and digest” phase now and hopefully this means more two way action than we’ve seen the last week as ES builds a new consolidation or corrects more. As always though - we don’t predict. We just plan zones and react. One speculative range for this new consolidation could be 7402 to 7340-45, but this is very early and the downside support of this range could easily end up far lower. 7340-45 is first support down. We’ve already engaged this extensively holding it at 1:20PM and 2:40PM, forming a shelf. The Failed Breakdown of this shelf is an actionable long. 7327 is below there. This is a very obvious support shelf from Monday that we defended 2x on Monday morning before rallying to the weekly highs and the flush and recovery of this shelf is an actionable Failed Breakdown. Nothing below until 7311. This is a good support. If it is a slower grind down into this zone, one can try the bid there. If we are knifing into it full speed though, no knife catching allowed. If we are knifing full power, a safer option is the Failed Breakdown of 7306. From midnight until 420AM Wednesday ES set a nice shelf of lows at 7306 and sweeps and recoveries of this shelf are actionable. 7279 is below there but if we are knifing full power don’t just instantly buy this - wait for it to hold then recover 7283 to get long. Nothing below there until 7257.

raw_mancini_source.quote: Bull case tomorrow: ES is now in the process of building a highly complex consolidation since April 17. This structure was mostly 7080 support with 7180 resistance. On Friday we broke this structure out. Then all day Monday, 7180 flipped to support, and we ripped to ~7225. Today, we then lost 7180 and it flipped back to resistance much of the session. The bull case tomorrow is that (in the most extreme form) bulls want to hold 7147 support (or quick trap below). From there, bulls want to reclaim 7180. Targets then become 7186, 7204, 7214, then 7234, then 7246. Given tomorrows catalysts, price is very unlikely to follow a clean path and will rather trap over and over again - again if have not mastered not predicting price, take tomorrow off. Once the session starts, enter pure reaction mode and do not predict anything. In terms of spots to add on strength, 7180 reclaims are a possible entry. We have extensively accepted it today with one backtest at 950AM and another at around 3pm.
raw_mancini_source.surrounding_source_context:

Generally this consolidation formed a large range with 7080 support (this was the Sunday low and Tuesday low last week) and 7180 resistance (this was a downsloping trendline connecting the last Friday high, last Tuesday high, and last Thursday high). As stated above last Thursday ES swept support of that range, trapped bears, then ripped the other direction back up the range to mildly break out on Friday. On Monday, resistance of this range at 7180 flipped to support, then we ran to 7225. Yesterday, we failed back into the range, and 7180 acted as resistance all day. Heading into today, I discussed at 4pm yesterday what bulls need to see:

We have extensively accepted it today with one backtest at 950AM and another at around 3pm.
1208: 
1209: 3) Finally, while I discuss plenty of structures and levels above, its vital that we do not use any of the above to make predictions. Our job as traders is never to predict what price will do (literally impossible on an intraday basis). Our job is to plan zones, wait for price to present our setups at those zones, then manage the trade systematically level to level. I am not exagerating at all when I say the core difference between winning and losing traders is that losing traders are obssessed with knowing where price will go. Professionals don’t care - they let price pick the path and react. This allows one to trade what they see, not what they think, and not be affected by bias. When you predict, you are giving yourself a bias which blinds you from objectively trading the action. Price traps by its nature and takes the pathway engineered to cause the maximum pain to retail traders. You can pretend that it is a supercomputer that is intentionally trying to ensure that no retail traders profit from any large move, and ensure they are all stopped out, trapped, and frustrated before one occurs.

raw_mancini_source.quote: Bull case tomorrow: The bull case has fully played out today and we are closing at all time highs an high of day. There is therefore not much to say here. Today’s rip was - as always - caused by a Failed Breakdown after earnings yesterday of 7137 which was yesterday’s daily low. Today’s rip also broke ES out of a range we had built all week which - as of today - mostly has resistance at 7188/7198, and support down at 7135. In a bull case, bulls want to hold that on any dips tomorrow/this evening. It is fine if price fails back into this, it simply means we need to build more structure, but in a strong bull case ES will defend this. Next slate of targets are 7252, 7267, 7297. I usually give spots to add on strength but now that we have broken out there is literally nothing to do but wait for a vertical drop.

792: Trade Plan Monday
793: 
794: Supports are: 7264 (major), 7257, 7253, 7248 (major), 7241, 7237 (major), 7228, 7220, 7212 (major), 7205, 7198 (major), 7194, 7188, 7181 (major), 7172, 7165, 7160 (major), 7153, 7153, 7147 (major), 7142 (major), 7135, 7129, 7121 (major), 7111, 7103, 7094, 7086 (major), 7080, 7074 (major), 7063, 7058, 7054 (major), 7049 (major), 7041, 7034, 7025 (major), 7021, 7016 (major), 7008, 6999, 6690 (major), 6984, 6975, 6962, 6949 (major), 6938 (major), 6927, 6920, 6907, 6903 (major).
795: 
796: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 1:50PM last Thursday Failed Breakdown of 7085 - discussed extensively in Thursday’s newsletter and provided in advance. My most recent entry was the 4:20PM 7137 Failed breakdown this Wednesday, provided in advance and reviewed in Wednesday’s newsletter. This week was a testament to why runners are so important in ES. When I got long at 4:20PM Wednesday after the major Failed Breakdown post-earnings of 7137, did I know we were about to go 150 points straight up? I am always bullish in an uptrend, but nobody ever knows the exact path higher price will take. Fortunately we don’t need to. We just manage level to level, let the runner work. The main benefit of runners is they control FOMO. When you have a runner, you don’t feel the urge to chase, or the urge to rush in. You have exposure. Price keeps going up? Great - runner works. Price goes elevator down - great, we get another entry. Unfortunately though, readers also know what I’m going to say here. We had yet another monster run today and while we did cool off from the highs, there are just no real quality, confident entries until we get a proper elevator down sell. 7264 is 1st support down. This has been support all afternoon. I would not buy this again, but at 240PM we set a low at 7259. Sweeps of this low and recoveries are a possible low quality Failed Breakdown. I am always cautious the day after a large squeeze for two reasons. Both pertain to the fact that price needs to digest a large move like this. This can occur in two ways: 1) A red day. If this occurs, every nearby support will fail after perhaps a quick scalp. 2) Price could build out a consolidation (this will probably be in a range between 7248 and 7300). This is fine for us day traders since we take profits level to level. Early on the digestion process, we don’t know which it will be which is why I take things very light. 7248 is below there. There is a nice shelf at 7248 and at 5am we set a big low there from which we rallied 50+ points. The Failed Breakdown of this is actionable. If that fails we probably start a nice retracement finally. Watch for Failed Breakdowns of 7237 (take profits fast on this one) and not much under there until 7212. If its a slow, controlled grind into 7212 one can try a bid here but remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed. If we are selling vertically you never long. 7198 is below there, this is also a decent support and it roughly backtests the week long range we broke out Thursday. If we are full speed knifing down, the safe entry here is to wait for price to flush through that, and also flush 7193. At 1240pm Thursday ES set a clear major low here and if you wait for that low to sweep and recover its much safer than just buying 7198 and hoping for the best. Nothing under there until 7160 and Failed breakdowns of 7160 are an excellent long. There is a very nice two touch set of lows at 7157-61. There is the 520AM low at 7167 from which we ripped to the morning highs Thursday, then there is the 7157 low from which we ripped to the current highs.

154: Trade Plan Friday
155: 
156: Supports are: 7355, 7348, 7345-40 (major), 7338, 7332, 7327 (major), 7320, 7311 (major), 7306, 7301, 7291, 7284, 7279 (major), 7274, 7263, 7257 (major), 7247, 7241, 7238 (major), 7228, 7220 (major), 7212 (major), 7207, 7200 (major), 7193, 7188 (major), 7181, 7173 (major), 7165, 7161 (major), 7153, 7147 (major), 7142, 7135 (major), 7128, 7121, 7111 (major), 7105, 7095 (major), 7085, 7080 (major), 7073, 7067, 7058 (major), 7048, 7041 (major), 7031, 7026, 7016-20 (major), 7007, 7002, 6991 (major), 6983, 6974, 6962 (major), 6955.
157: 
158: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 12:20PM 7213 Failed breakdown on Monday. My most recent entry was the 7255 Failed Breakdown at noon today. Today we finally got some red and as a result we saw setups for the first time since Monday. I said this yesterday, and it was applicable today: “Readers know I always say that while I only trade the long side, I infinitely prefer either red or sideways markets to rising markets. Why? Because as a day trader, I get paid on volatility, not direction. Red and sideways markets are more volatile. More volatile means more Failed Breakdowns, and larger squeezes. On a very green day or week, there may be 2 or setups all week. On a red day (like Monday) we sometimes get 3 in a day, often more. Yes on a real red day (which we inevitably get soon), those Failed Breakdowns may only pop 10, 20, 50 points before rejecting down. This is fine - we got paid, and usually there are several opportunities to do so on a good red day.” After this weeks parabolic rally ES is likely into a “rest and digest” phase now and hopefully this means more two way action than we’ve seen the last week as ES builds a new consolidation or corrects more. As always though - we don’t predict. We just plan zones and react. One speculative range for this new consolidation could be 7402 to 7340-45, but this is very early and the downside support of this range could easily end up far lower. 7340-45 is first support down. We’ve already engaged this extensively holding it at 1:20PM and 2:40PM, forming a shelf. The Failed Breakdown of this shelf is an actionable long. 7327 is below there. This is a very obvious support shelf from Monday that we defended 2x on Monday morning before rallying to the weekly highs and the flush and recovery of this shelf is an actionable Failed Breakdown. Nothing below until 7311. This is a good support. If it is a slower grind down into this zone, one can try the bid there. If we are knifing into it full speed though, no knife catching allowed. If we are knifing full power, a safer option is the Failed Breakdown of 7306. From midnight until 420AM Wednesday ES set a nice shelf of lows at 7306 and sweeps and recoveries of this shelf are actionable. 7279 is below there but if we are knifing full power don’t just instantly buy this - wait for it to hold then recover 7283 to get long. Nothing below there until 7257.

796: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 1:50PM last Thursday Failed Breakdown of 7085 - discussed extensively in Thursday’s newsletter and provided in advance. My most recent entry was the 4:20PM 7137 Failed breakdown this Wednesday, provided in advance and reviewed in Wednesday’s newsletter. This week was a testament to why runners are so important in ES. When I got long at 4:20PM Wednesday after the major Failed Breakdown post-earnings of 7137, did I know we were about to go 150 points straight up? I am always bullish in an uptrend, but nobody ever knows the exact path higher price will take. Fortunately we don’t need to. We just manage level to level, let the runner work. The main benefit of runners is they control FOMO. When you have a runner, you don’t feel the urge to chase, or the urge to rush in. You have exposure. Price keeps going up? Great - runner works. Price goes elevator down - great, we get another entry. Unfortunately though, readers also know what I’m going to say here. We had yet another monster run today and while we did cool off from the highs, there are just no real quality, confident entries until we get a proper elevator down sell. 7264 is 1st support down. This has been support all afternoon. I would not buy this again, but at 240PM we set a low at 7259. Sweeps of this low and recoveries are a possible low quality Failed Breakdown. I am always cautious the day after a large squeeze for two reasons. Both pertain to the fact that price needs to digest a large move like this. This can occur in two ways: 1) A red day. If this occurs, every nearby support will fail after perhaps a quick scalp. 2) Price could build out a consolidation (this will probably be in a range between 7248 and 7300). This is fine for us day traders since we take profits level to level. Early on the digestion process, we don’t know which it will be which is why I take things very light. 7248 is below there. There is a nice shelf at 7248 and at 5am we set a big low there from which we rallied 50+ points. The Failed Breakdown of this is actionable. If that fails we probably start a nice retracement finally. Watch for Failed Breakdowns of 7237 (take profits fast on this one) and not much under there until 7212. If its a slow, controlled grind into 7212 one can try a bid here but remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed. If we are selling vertically you never long. 7198 is below there, this is also a decent support and it roughly backtests the week long range we broke out Thursday. If we are full speed knifing down, the safe entry here is to wait for price to flush through that, and also flush 7193. At 1240pm Thursday ES set a clear major low here and if you wait for that low to sweep and recover its much safer than just buying 7198 and hoping for the best. Nothing under there until 7160 and Failed breakdowns of 7160 are an excellent long. There is a very nice two touch set of lows at 7157-61. There is the 520AM low at 7167 from which we ripped to the morning highs Thursday, then there is the 7157 low from which we ripped to the current highs.

158: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 12:20PM 7213 Failed breakdown on Monday. My most recent entry was the 7255 Failed Breakdown at noon today. Today we finally got some red and as a result we saw setups for the first time since Monday. I said this yesterday, and it was applicable today: “Readers know I always say that while I only trade the long side, I infinitely prefer either red or sideways markets to rising markets. Why? Because as a day trader, I get paid on volatility, not direction. Red and sideways markets are more volatile. More volatile means more Failed Breakdowns, and larger squeezes. On a very green day or week, there may be 2 or setups all week. On a red day (like Monday) we sometimes get 3 in a day, often more. Yes on a real red day (which we inevitably get soon), those Failed Breakdowns may only pop 10, 20, 50 points before rejecting down. This is fine - we got paid, and usually there are several opportunities to do so on a good red day.” After this weeks parabolic rally ES is likely into a “rest and digest” phase now and hopefully this means more two way action than we’ve seen the last week as ES builds a new consolidation or corrects more. As always though - we don’t predict. We just plan zones and react. One speculative range for this new consolidation could be 7402 to 7340-45, but this is very early and the downside support of this range could easily end up far lower. 7340-45 is first support down. We’ve already engaged this extensively holding it at 1:20PM and 2:40PM, forming a shelf. The Failed Breakdown of this shelf is an actionable long. 7327 is below there. This is a very obvious support shelf from Monday that we defended 2x on Monday morning before rallying to the weekly highs and the flush and recovery of this shelf is an actionable Failed Breakdown. Nothing below until 7311. This is a good support. If it is a slower grind down into this zone, one can try the bid there. If we are knifing into it full speed though, no knife catching allowed. If we are knifing full power, a safer option is the Failed Breakdown of 7306. From midnight until 420AM Wednesday ES set a nice shelf of lows at 7306 and sweeps and recoveries of this shelf are actionable. 7279 is below there but if we are knifing full power don’t just instantly buy this - wait for it to hold then recover 7283 to get long. Nothing below there until 7257.

raw_mancini_source.quote: Bull case tomorrow: The bull case has fully played out today and we are closing at all time highs an high of day. There is therefore not much to say here. Today’s rip was - as always - caused by a Failed Breakdown after earnings yesterday of 7137 which was yesterday’s daily low. Today’s rip also broke ES out of a range we had built all week which - as of today - mostly has resistance at 7188/7198, and support down at 7135. In a bull case, bulls want to hold that on any dips tomorrow/this evening. It is fine if price fails back into this, it simply means we need to build more structure, but in a strong bull case ES will defend this. Next slate of targets are 7252, 7267, 7297. I usually give spots to add on strength but now that we have broken out there is literally nothing to do but wait for a vertical drop.

798: Resistances are: 7275, 7285 (major), 7296, 7300, 7308 (major), 7313, 7323 (major), 7329, 7337 (major), 7350, 7355 (major), 7365, 7371 (major), 7382, 7387 (major), 7395, 7403, 7411 (major), 7415 (major), 7422, 7427, 7440-43 (major). As readers know I don’t short ES - I only get my points on the long side, but I still give short entries here for those who like them and don’t mind having a substantially lower win rate and lower R/R - SPX is a structurally bullish asset class green 75% of years over the last 100 years with a 1% annualized monthly return and even in bear markets it is extremely hard to short since the sells are short lived, news driven (often random) and resolve in violent rip. I have not had a single short in over a year now - phasing them out after 15 years - and my performance has never been better as my Failed Breakdown strategy outperforms even the strongest shorting strategies in downtrends and uptrends. Hopefully this recent leg up will remind people why I don’t short ES - but I don’t even short even in the strongest downtrends. For those who don’t mind the 10-30% win rate associated with shorting an ES uptrend though, 7285 is one spot to try, as is 7323 and 7337.

795: 
796: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 1:50PM last Thursday Failed Breakdown of 7085 - discussed extensively in Thursday’s newsletter and provided in advance. My most recent entry was the 4:20PM 7137 Failed breakdown this Wednesday, provided in advance and reviewed in Wednesday’s newsletter. This week was a testament to why runners are so important in ES. When I got long at 4:20PM Wednesday after the major Failed Breakdown post-earnings of 7137, did I know we were about to go 150 points straight up? I am always bullish in an uptrend, but nobody ever knows the exact path higher price will take. Fortunately we don’t need to. We just manage level to level, let the runner work. The main benefit of runners is they control FOMO. When you have a runner, you don’t feel the urge to chase, or the urge to rush in. You have exposure. Price keeps going up? Great - runner works. Price goes elevator down - great, we get another entry. Unfortunately though, readers also know what I’m going to say here. We had yet another monster run today and while we did cool off from the highs, there are just no real quality, confident entries until we get a proper elevator down sell. 7264 is 1st support down. This has been support all afternoon. I would not buy this again, but at 240PM we set a low at 7259. Sweeps of this low and recoveries are a possible low quality Failed Breakdown. I am always cautious the day after a large squeeze for two reasons. Both pertain to the fact that price needs to digest a large move like this. This can occur in two ways: 1) A red day. If this occurs, every nearby support will fail after perhaps a quick scalp. 2) Price could build out a consolidation (this will probably be in a range between 7248 and 7300). This is fine for us day traders since we take profits level to level. Early on the digestion process, we don’t know which it will be which is why I take things very light. 7248 is below there. There is a nice shelf at 7248 and at 5am we set a big low there from which we rallied 50+ points. The Failed Breakdown of this is actionable. If that fails we probably start a nice retracement finally. Watch for Failed Breakdowns of 7237 (take profits fast on this one) and not much under there until 7212. If its a slow, controlled grind into 7212 one can try a bid here but remember my core rule in ES: No knife catching allowed. If we are selling vertically you never long. 7198 is below there, this is also a decent support and it roughly backtests the week long range we broke out Thursday. If we are full speed knifing down, the safe entry here is to wait for price to flush through that, and also flush 7193. At 1240pm Thursday ES set a clear major low here and if you wait for that low to sweep and recover its much safer than just buying 7198 and hoping for the best. Nothing under there until 7160 and Failed breakdowns of 7160 are an excellent long. There is a very nice two touch set of lows at 7157-61. There is the 520AM low at 7167 from which we ripped to the morning highs Thursday, then there is the 7157 low from which we ripped to the current highs.

158: In terms of lvls I’d bid direct: I am still holding my 10% long runner from the 12:20PM 7213 Failed breakdown on Monday. My most recent entry was the 7255 Failed Breakdown at noon today. Today we finally got some red and as a result we saw setups for the first time since Monday. I said this yesterday, and it was applicable today: “Readers know I always say that while I only trade the long side, I infinitely prefer either red or sideways markets to rising markets. Why? Because as a day trader, I get paid on volatility, not direction. Red and sideways markets are more volatile. More volatile means more Failed Breakdowns, and larger squeezes. On a very green day or week, there may be 2 or setups all week. On a red day (like Monday) we sometimes get 3 in a day, often more. Yes on a real red day (which we inevitably get soon), those Failed Breakdowns may only pop 10, 20, 50 points before rejecting down. This is fine - we got paid, and usually there are several opportunities to do so on a good red day.” After this weeks parabolic rally ES is likely into a “rest and digest” phase now and hopefully this means more two way action than we’ve seen the last week as ES builds a new consolidation or corrects more. As always though - we don’t predict. We just plan zones and react. One speculative range for this new consolidation could be 7402 to 7340-45, but this is very early and the downside support of this range could easily end up far lower. 7340-45 is first support down. We’ve already engaged this extensively holding it at 1:20PM and 2:40PM, forming a shelf. The Failed Breakdown of this shelf is an actionable long. 7327 is below there. This is a very obvious support shelf from Monday that we defended 2x on Monday morning before rallying to the weekly highs and the flush and recovery of this shelf is an actionable Failed Breakdown. Nothing below until 7311. This is a good support. If it is a slower grind down into this zone, one can try the bid there. If we are knifing into it full speed though, no knife catching allowed. If we are knifing full power, a safer option is the Failed Breakdown of 7306. From midnight until 420AM Wednesday ES set a nice shelf of lows at 7306 and sweeps and recoveries of this shelf are actionable. 7279 is below there but if we are knifing full power don’t just instantly buy this - wait for it to hold then recover 7283 to get long. Nothing below there until 7257.

raw_mancini_source.quote: When I woke up and checked price at 730AM, I noticed we got a nice elevator down sell at 6am from 7266 to 7213. If you want to see what an elevator down sell looks like - this is what they look like. A fast flush that cuts every support and often covers 20-200 points in no time at all. There is a sentiment component as well, if you are on social media you will see retail traders (95-99% of who lose at day trading according to multiple studies) panicking, top calling etc. This is the precondition for a Failed Breakdown.
